title: Effects of Booking Horizon Reduction on Cancellation Rates
short_summary: This work studied how shortening the time window for booking appointments affects last-minute cancellations and no-shows in an outpatient pediatric rehabilitation setting. Using real-world clinic data around a policy change, the paper shows that reducing the booking horizon can meaningfully improve attendance and make scheduling more reliable.

title: Reducing Diagnostic Imaging Wait Times: Lessons from a Large-Scale Empirical Analysis
short_summary: This work examines how day-to-day scheduling decisions in MRI departments—especially how procedures are sequenced throughout the day—affect scan durations and overall throughput. Using large-scale hospital data, the paper shows that smarter sequencing and workload-aware scheduling can meaningfully increase effective capacity and help reduce diagnostic imaging wait times without adding resources.

title: The Impact of Treatment Modaity and Waiting Time
short_summary: This work investigates how operational choices in multi-visit outpatient care—like how long patients wait to start treatment and whether visits are delivered in person or via telemedicine—shape both care intensity and appointment attendance. Using pediatric speech-language therapy data, the paper shows how these factors influence how many visits patients ultimately need to reach discharge, highlighting levers to improve efficiency and patient outcomes.
